learning_objectives: Explain the mechanism of analgesia for locoregional anesthesia, neuraxial analgesia, electroacupuncture, and photobiomodulation
Select anatomically appropriate nerve branches for common companion animal surgical procedures including TECA-BO
Calculate maximum safe local anesthetic doses and recognize signs of local anesthetic systemic toxicity (LAST)
Apply photobiomodulation delivery parameters including wavelength range (635-904 nm) and cytochrome C oxidase as the primary intracellular target
Distinguish electroacupuncture frequency-dependent mediator release (2 Hz vs 100 Hz)
Identify contraindications and complete safety screening for each technique modality before use
Integrate techniques into multimodal perioperative analgesia plans with mechanistic rationale
Recognize scope-of-competence limits and describe credentialing pathways for new techniques
high_priority_objectives: Select anatomically appropriate nerve branches for common companion animal surgical procedures including TECA-BO
Identify contraindications and complete safety screening for each technique modality before use
mastery_threshold_note: Fellows must demonstrate specific knowledge of delivery parameters, safety screening, and technique selection rationale. Generic mechanism descriptions without clinical application indicate insufficient mastery.
